rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KEIKO M. JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The Court will inquire about DFAS-Cleveland pension payment. It is labeled as "pension"; however, the monthly payment stopped in February 2024. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENNY YEE KUN NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders 1) Determining Title To Personal Property; 2)Removal Of Special Administrator; 3) Suspending Powers Of Personal Representative Pending Resolution Of This Petition; 4) Appointing Private Professional Fiduciary As Special Administrator And 5) Directing Special Administrator To Account
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/26/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearin (DE-115), on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to the proof of service. A petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 requires 30 days' notice. Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide a courtesy copy of the proof of service of the notice of the 8/26/26 hearing to the assigned examiner by 8/12/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT E. O'DONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/26. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proposed distributions to the beneficiaries of the pecuniary bequests show the daily interest for just 671 days, which would have been for distribution by 12/21/25, and not through the date of this hearing and including the amount payable each day until distributed. It appears that the reserve amount requested might not be sufficient to cover the interest (?). (2) Are the financial statements that were filed on 6/18/26 (no courtesy copy was received by the examiner as of 6/29/26) originals that Executor has in his possession and could be presented to the Court if required? See LRSF 14.34 D. and CRC 7.575(b). (3) First Citizens Bank appears to be a North Carolina bank, not a California bank nor a national bank. [Probate Code Section 9700.] (4) There is no informational schedule attached to the accounting, but the 462 shares of GE were apparently split into two different entities. (5) The number of shares of each asset sold is not shown on the gains/losses schedule. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/8/26. (6) Fees paid to another to prepare the court accounting must be deducted from the personal representative's statutory compensation. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/8/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement or an amendment should stand on its own and not respond to issues noted by an examiner; the issues are not part of the filed record.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOAN COPANS BENJAMIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving And Confirming All Acts Of Conservator Of Person; And, Awarding Conservator?S And Attorneys? Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/26. (No earlier availability.) No response to issues posted for the hearing on 6/8/26. The fee petition has not yet been fully reviewed as no courtesy copy of the petition and any supporting documents (fee declarations or proofs of service) have been received by the assigned examiner as of 6/29/26. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Have the attorneys and the guardian ad litem consented in writing to receiving notice electronically? The examiner has been unable to find the consents scrolling through the register of actions online. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOAN COPANS BENJAMIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/26. (No earlier availability.) Examiner needs more time to review the second and third amendments to the accounting petition, the first amendment to the attorney fee declaration, and the first amendment to the financial statements. (1) The amendments filed on 6/17/26 were not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, the copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to the proof of service. (3) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (It would have been better to file a fully amended accounting petition instead of all these piecemeal filings that make reviewing them very challenging. The Court has no jurisdiction over the trust; any reference to the Court ordering the trustee to act will be disregarded.) Deadline is 8/17/26 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH ANNE MORRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/26. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended petition was not verified. [Probate Code Section 1021.] Deadline is 7/6/26 to provide the assigned examiner a courtesy copy of the verification of the amended petition. Courtesy copies are FILE STAMPED, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (An amended pleading completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3).] There is no reason to refer to items noted in tentative rulings; an amended petition should stand on its own as with the original petition. Paragraph 10 only mentions the sale of the real property but does not disclose what Administrator did with the other assets, but there is no longer any tangible personal property and the funds reported on the I&A are not the ones now on hand. It does not seem likely that there was any personal property tax due and payable by this estate.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLYN C. BELT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/03/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Decedent's will leaves the residue of the estate to the Carolyn C. Belt Revocable Trust. If the person entitled to distribution is a trust, a waiver shall be executed by the trustee, but only if the named trustee's written acceptance of trust is filed with the court (Probate Code Section 10954(b)(4)). 2) The instant petition requests outright distribution to petitioners, although the will leaves the residue of the estate to decedent's revocable trust. Please provide authority for distribution of the residue that is not in accordance with decedent's will. Please provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 07/20/2026 to avoid further continuance.
